name: Ph.D. in Environmental Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The five referenced courses (ENE 725/726 Independent Study I/II, ENE 791 Graduate Seminar at 0 credits, ENE 792 Pre-Doctoral Research) form a coherent research spine of independent study to seminar to pre-doctoral research suited to a doctorate, but no environmental coursework depth (treatment, remediation, air/water quality) is surfaced, so the page conveys the research milestones without the disciplinary substance of the field.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the required advanced environmental coursework (physical/chemical and biological treatment, remediation, environmental chemistry/microbiology) and the qualifying/candidacy milestones alongside the ENE research vehicles.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 with no course prerequisites on the visible research courses, which is expected for a doctorate gated by advisor approval and qualifying exams (ENE 792 co-requisite with ENE 791); the single missingCourseCount is ENE 790A, the dissertation/research course, so the one dangling reference is the culminating vehicle.
dimensions.suggestion: Add ENE 790A to the catalog so the culminating research course resolves, and document qualifying-exam prerequisites rather than leaving milestones ungated.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, so applicants cannot see the research and technical competencies (independent investigation, environmental analysis, scholarly dissemination) the doctorate certifies.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-5 Ph.D. learning outcomes describing original environmental-research, methodological, and communication competencies.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 12 with creditReconcileDelta null; those 12 credits reflect the research/seminar block shown rather than the full doctoral total (typically 60+ post-B.S. or ~30+ post-M.S.), so the coursework-versus-research balance is not transparent.
dimensions.suggestion: State the full doctoral credit requirement and its split among required coursework, dissertation research (ENE 792/790A), and seminar.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The four visible ENE courses have clear administrative descriptions (registration approvals, seminar format, pre-doctoral research scope), but there is no plan of study, no labeled candidacy stages, and no outcomes, so the page is readable but incomplete.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a plan of study with candidacy, proposal, and defense stages and a brief program overview above the research-course list.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The visible courses are content-neutral research vehicles (independent study, seminar, pre-doctoral research), so disciplinary currency cannot be read from the page; the doctorate's relevance resides in faculty research areas not surfaced here.
dimensions.suggestion: List active environmental research thrusts (water reuse, emerging contaminants/PFAS, climate and sustainability, environmental biotechnology) so currency is visible.

descriptionQuality.justification: The four descriptions present are clear and specific about registration approvals, seminar format, and the scope of pre-doctoral research, but they are administrative rather than content-bearing, conveying process more than environmental substance.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated as advisor/instructor approvals and a co-requisite (ENE 792 with ENE 791), which is unambiguous and internally consistent; the sole gap is the dangling ENE 790A reference.

missingTopics.rationale: Only ENE research vehicles (725/726/791/792) appear; the required treatment, remediation, and environmental-chemistry depth that defines the doctorate is not surfaced.
Contemporary environmental doctoral research centers on trace/emerging contaminants, none of which is visible in the referenced courses.
Statistical and process modeling of environmental systems is a core doctoral competency absent from the page.
ENE 792 pre-doctoral research is shown but no qualifying-exam or candidacy milestone, leaving doctoral gating undocumented.

emergingTrends.rationale: Destruction and removal of persistent contaminants is a top environmental-engineering research frontier the doctorate should position for.
Potable reuse and integrated urban water are rapidly growing research areas aligned with the department's water strengths.
Environmental research increasingly targets carbon and climate, a thrust the doctorate should surface explicitly.
Omics-driven understanding of environmental microbial processes is an emerging doctoral direction complementing treatment research.

topSuggestions: Surface the required advanced environmental coursework, qualifying exam, and candidacy milestones so the page shows the full doctoral progression, not only the ENE research vehicles.
Publish 4-5 Ph.D. learning outcomes describing original-research and dissemination competencies.
Resolve the dangling ENE 790A dissertation/research reference in the catalog.
State the full doctoral credit total and its coursework-versus-research split so the 12-credit figure is not read as the whole degree.
List active environmental research thrusts (PFAS/emerging contaminants, water reuse, climate) so the doctorate's currency is visible.
